members.name: AddDoMethod
members.summary: Register a method that will be called when the action is committed (i.e. the "do" action). If this is the first operation, the [param object] will be used to deduce target undo history.

members.name: AddDoProperty
members.signature: public void AddDoProperty(Object @object, StringName property, Variant value)
members.summary: Register a property value change for "do". If this is the first operation, the [param object] will be used to deduce target undo history.

members.name: AddDoReference
members.summary: Register a reference for "do" that will be erased if the "do" history is lost. This is useful mostly for new nodes created for the "do" call. Do not use for resources.

members.name: AddUndoMethod
members.summary: Register a method that will be called when the action is undone (i.e. the "undo" action). If this is the first operation, the [param object] will be used to deduce target undo history.

members.name: AddUndoProperty
members.signature: public void AddUndoProperty(Object @object, StringName property, Variant value)
members.summary: Register a property value change for "undo". If this is the first operation, the [param object] will be used to deduce target undo history.

members.name: AddUndoReference
members.summary: Register a reference for "undo" that will be erased if the "undo" history is lost. This is useful mostly for nodes removed with the "do" call (not the "undo" call!).

members.name: ClearHistory
members.signature: public void ClearHistory(int id, bool increaseVersion)
members.summary: Clears the given undo history. You can clear history for a specific scene, global history, or for all histories at once (except [constant REMOTE_HISTORY]) if [param id] is [constant INVALID_HISTORY]. If [param increase_version] is [code]true[/code], the undo history version will be increased, marking it as unsaved. Useful for operations that modify the scene, but don't support undo. [codeblock] var scene_root = EditorInterface.get_edited_scene_root() var undo_redo = EditorInterface.get_editor_undo_redo() undo_redo.clear_history(undo_redo.get_object_history_id(scene_root)) [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] If you want to mark an edited scene as unsaved without clearing its history, use [method EditorInterface.mark_scene_as_unsaved] instead.

members.name: CommitAction
members.summary: Commits the action. If [param execute] is [code]true[/code] (default), all "do" methods/properties are called/set when this function is called.

members.name: CreateAction
members.signature: public void CreateAction(String name, int mergeMode, Object customContext, bool backwardUndoOps, bool markUnsaved)
members.summary: Create a new action. After this is called, do all your calls to [method add_do_method], [method add_undo_method], [method add_do_property], and [method add_undo_property], then commit the action with [method commit_action]. The way actions are merged is dictated by the [param merge_mode] argument. If [param custom_context] object is provided, it will be used for deducing target history (instead of using the first operation). The way undo operation are ordered in actions is dictated by [param backward_undo_ops]. When [param backward_undo_ops] is [code]false[/code] undo option are ordered in the same order they were added. Which means the first operation to be added will be the first to be undone. If [param mark_unsaved] is [code]false[/code], the action will not mark the history as unsaved. This is useful for example for actions that change a selection, or a setting that will be saved automatically. Otherwise, this should be left to [code]true[/code] if the action requires saving by the user or if it can cause data loss when left unsaved.

members.name: ForceFixedHistory
members.summary: Forces the next operation (e.g. [method add_do_method]) to use the action's history rather than guessing it from the object. This is sometimes needed when a history can't be correctly determined, like for a nested resource that doesn't have a path yet. This method should only be used when absolutely necessary, otherwise it might cause invalid history state. For most of complex cases, the [code]custom_context[/code] parameter of [method create_action] is sufficient.

members.name: GetHistoryUndoRedo
members.summary: Returns the [UndoRedo] object associated with the given history [param id]. [param id] above [code]0[/code] are mapped to the opened scene tabs (but it doesn't match their order). [param id] of [code]0[/code] or lower have special meaning (see [enum SpecialHistory]). Best used with [method get_object_history_id]. This method is only provided in case you need some more advanced methods of [UndoRedo] (but keep in mind that directly operating on the [UndoRedo] object might affect editor's stability).

members.name: GetObjectHistoryId
members.summary: Returns the history ID deduced from the given [param object]. It can be used with [method get_history_undo_redo].

members.name: IsCommittingAction
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [EditorUndoRedoManager] is currently committing the action, i.e. running its "do" method or property change (see [method commit_action]).

signals.name: HistoryChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the list of actions in any history has changed, either when an action is committed or a history is cleared.

signals.name: VersionChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the version of any history has changed as a result of undo or redo call.

values.name: GLOBAL_HISTORY
values.summary: Global history not associated with any scene, but with external resources etc.

values.name: INVALID_HISTORY
values.summary: Invalid "null" history. It's a special value, not associated with any object.

values.name: REMOTE_HISTORY
values.summary: History associated with remote inspector. Used when live editing a running project.

constants.name: GLOBAL_HISTORY
constants.summary: Global history not associated with any scene, but with external resources etc.

constants.name: REMOTE_HISTORY
constants.summary: History associated with remote inspector. Used when live editing a running project.

constants.name: INVALID_HISTORY
constants.summary: Invalid "null" history. It's a special value, not associated with any object.
